metadata.author: Juan Antonio Breña Moral
metadata.title: Micronaut — Kafka messaging
metadata.description: Use when you need Kafka in Micronaut and want examples for design, implementation, and error handling with @KafkaClient and @KafkaListener.

prompt.role: You are a Senior software engineer with extensive experience in Micronaut and Kafka
prompt.goal: Design and implement reliable Micronaut Kafka flows with explicit contracts and resilient consumer error handling.

constraints.constraints-description: Compile first; stop on failure; verify after changes.

example.example-description: Configure Kafka bootstrap servers and application names properly. Ensure consumer groups are unique per logical application to avoid accidental load balancing across unrelated services.

good-example.code-block: # Good: explicit bootstrap servers and application name kafka: bootstrap: servers: localhost:9092 micronaut: application: name: billing-service # topic: orders.events.v1 # group: billing-service-v1

bad-example.code-block: # Bad: same consumer group reused by unrelated services causing accidental load balancing app.kafka.group: shared-group

example.example-description: Use `@KafkaClient` interfaces to produce messages declaratively. Use `@KafkaListener` to consume messages, specifying the `groupId`. Always use typed payloads.

example-header.example-subtitle: Retry with backoff and dead-letter strategy

example.example-description: Use `errorStrategy = KafkaListenerExceptionHandlerValue.RETRY_ON_ERROR` to automatically retry failed messages. Never swallow exceptions, as it acknowledges poison messages.
Start Kafka in Testcontainers and inject broker properties through `TestPropertyProvider` for reproducible integration tests.

bad-example.code-block: // Bad: test uses hardcoded localhost:9092 and is not CI-portable class BillingKafkaLocalEnvIT {}

output-format-list.output-format-item: **ANALYZE** messaging code: `@KafkaClient`/`@KafkaListener` usage, topic naming, serialization, and error handling strategies
**APPLY** Micronaut Kafka-aligned fixes: configure proper error strategies, use typed payloads, ensure idempotency
**IMPLEMENT** changes consistently across `application.yml` and Java code
**EXPLAIN** trade-offs (e.g., retry vs DLQ, consumer group isolation)
**VALIDATE** with `./mvnw compile` before and `./mvnw clean verify` after changes

safeguards-list.safeguards-item: **BLOCKING SAFETY CHECK**: Run `./mvnw compile` before ANY refactoring
**POISON PILLS**: Use `RETRY_ON_ERROR` or explicit exception handlers to prevent poison pills from silently being acknowledged
**CONSUMER GROUPS**: Always specify a `groupId` in `@KafkaListener` to prevent random group generation and message loss on restart
